skill.name: academic-cv-generator
skill.description: Generate structured academic CVs from free-form Chinese/English text and export to Word (.docx). Use this skill when you are asked to organize, generate, or optimize an academic CV (e.g., publications/projects/awards) into a consistent, formatted document with uniform-colored section headers and optional bilingual output.
skill.summary: Parses free-form CV text into structured academic sections and exports formatted Word documents with bilingual support.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 150 static analysis findings are false positives after human evaluation. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings (50+ instances) result from pattern-matching Unicode regex ranges and Python string methods unrelated to cryptography. The 'hard link creation' findings (25 instances) confuse Python string .lstrip() with filesystem link operations. The 'shell backtick execution' findings (26 instances) match documentation code blocks in SKILL.md, not actual command execution. The skill is a legitimate CV generation tool that reads text input and writes Word documents with no malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Scanner confusion: string lstrip misidentified as hard link creation
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged Python string .lstrip() calls (e.g., stripping BOM characters or whitespace) as filesystem hard link operations. These are standard string processing operations in a text-processing CV generator.

low_findings.title: Scanner confusion: Unicode regex ranges flagged as weak cryptography
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged Chinese character Unicode ranges [\u4e00-\u9fff] used in regex patterns as 'weak cryptographic algorithm'. These are legitimate character class patterns for detecting Chinese text in a bilingual CV tool.

low_findings.title: Scanner confusion: dict keys() flagged as certificate files
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged Python dictionary .keys() method calls as 'Certificate/key files'. These are ordinary dictionary operations, not cryptographic certificate handling.

low_findings.title: Documentation code blocks flagged as shell command execution
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged backtick-fenced code blocks in SKILL.md as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. These are markdown documentation examples showing users how to run the tool, not dynamic shell command execution.

low_findings.title: Source URL flagged as hardcoded network endpoint
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged the GitHub source repository URL in SKILL.md metadata as a 'Hardcoded URL'. This is the legitimate source reference for the open-source skill.

content.user_title: Generate Academic CVs from Free-Form Text
content.value_statement: Organizes messy CV drafts into structured academic CVs with proper sections for education, projects, publications, and awards. Exports formatted Word documents with consistent styling and automatic Chinese or English language detection.
actual_capabilities: Parses free-form Chinese and English text into standard academic sections
Classifies content into personal information, education, projects, publications, awards, and skills
Sorts education entries by degree level and project entries by date
Exports formatted Word (.docx) files with uniform color-coded section headers
Infers skills from project descriptions when explicit skills are not provided
Supports bilingual output with automatic language detection
limitations: Does not support PDF export, only Word (.docx) format
Cannot edit existing CVs incrementally; re-parses all input each time
Publication citation format is preserved as-is and not reformatted or validated
Skills inference from project descriptions may produce incomplete results

use_cases.title: Researcher CV Compilation
use_cases.description: A researcher with years of publications and projects needs a professional CV. The skill parses their free-form notes into organized sections and exports a formatted Word document.

use_cases.title: Bilingual Academic Profile
use_cases.description: A Chinese academic needs both Chinese and English versions of their CV. The skill detects the input language and formats the output accordingly.

use_cases.title: Graduate School Application
use_cases.description: A graduate student compiles application materials from scattered notes. The skill organizes education history, project experience, and publications into a clean document ready for submission.

prompt_templates.title: Basic CV Organization
prompt_templates.prompt:
Organize the following text into a professional academic CV. Create sections for personal information, education, projects, publications, and skills. Output as a Word document:

[paste CV text here]
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick formatting of clean input text

prompt_templates.title: Bilingual CV with Custom Color
prompt_templates.prompt:
Generate an academic CV from this text. Use English headers and cyan color for section titles. Include the awards section if any awards are mentioned:

[paste CV text here]
prompt_templates.scenario: Bilingual output with custom visual styling

prompt_templates.title: Publication-Focused CV
prompt_templates.prompt:
Create an academic CV from this researcher profile. Preserve all publication citations exactly as written without changing their format. Sort projects by most recent first:

[paste CV text here]

prompt_templates.title: Complete CV with All Sections
prompt_templates.prompt:
Parse this free-form CV draft into a structured academic CV. Include all sections: personal information, education sorted by degree level, projects sorted by date, publications, awards, and skills. Use random header color. Export as a Word document:

[paste CV text here]
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive CV generation with full section support

output_examples.input: A Chinese researcher provides a paragraph describing their PhD in Computer Science from Peking University, followed by three project descriptions and two publication titles in Chinese.
output_examples.output: The skill generates a Word document with: the researcher name as a bold 18pt header, a contact line with title and affiliation, an Education section with the PhD entry listed first, a Projects section with entries sorted by year, and a Publications section that preserves the original citation format.

output_examples.input: A graduate student submits a mixed-language CV with Chinese personal information and English publication titles.
output_examples.output: The skill detects the Chinese text, extracts name and university from the narrative paragraphs, and preserves English publication titles exactly. The output uses bullet-point formatting under each section with a uniform header color.

best_practices: Provide input with clear section indicators for best classification results
Verify extracted personal information such as name, title, and organization before finalizing
Review the generated skills section and add explicit skill entries if the inference is incomplete
anti_patterns: Do not include sensitive personal data beyond what is needed for a professional CV
Do not expect the skill to reformat publication citation styles or fix citation errors
Do not use the skill to generate CVs for other people without their consent

faq.question: What input formats does this skill accept?
faq.answer: It accepts free-form text in Chinese or English. Text with clear section headers works best, but narrative paragraphs without headers are also supported.

faq.question: Can I use this skill to update an existing CV?
faq.answer: Yes, but you must provide the full text each time. The skill re-parses all input from scratch rather than applying incremental edits.

faq.question: What output format is supported?
faq.answer: The skill exports to Word (.docx) format only. PDF conversion is not currently supported.

faq.question: How does the skill handle bilingual content?
faq.answer: It automatically detects whether the input is in Chinese or English. You can also specify the output language using the language selection option.

faq.question: Can I customize the document appearance?
faq.answer: Yes, you can choose a header color from purple, cyan, green, or red. A random option picks one automatically if you do not specify a color.

faq.question: What happens if the skills section is empty?
faq.answer: The skill infers skills from project descriptions when explicit skills are not provided. You should review the inferred list for accuracy and add any missing skills.
